description:
## Question

What does darwin-modules/features/voice.nix look like, and how do the SuperWhisper hooks merge into the Nix-managed Claude Code settings?

Design:
- The feature module (options.features.voice.enable + config): homebrew.casks = [ "superwhisper" ] when enabled, mirroring the music/llm/tailscale pattern; wired in darwin-modules/features/default.nix.
- Hook translation: port the reference's Stop / Notification / PreToolUse(AskUserQuestion) / PermissionRequest / UserPromptSubmit hooks (each invoking the claude-hook binary from ticket 1) into settings.hooks, which already carries WorktreeCreate/WorktreeRemove in home-modules/settings/programs/claude-code.nix.
- Composition: those settings are gated on config.programs.claude-code.enable; voice hooks must ALSO gate on the voice feature. Decide where the merge lives (extend the existing settings block vs a separate mkIf) so it degrades cleanly on Macs where claude-code is off (coupling = Independent).
- Whether the hook command references the app binary path directly or a nix wrapper.

HITL prototype (may stub the nix module).

comments.text:
RESOLVED (prototype/design). Full blueprint: docs/research/voice-darwin-module-design.md (committed nix-4t3.3). The real module lands in nix-4t3.6.

DESIGN:
1. Hook target = DIRECT /Applications path: command = '/Applications/superwhisper.app/Contents/Resources/claude-hook'. Cask guarantees the app there; no nix wrapper (a GUI-shipped shim can't be lib.getExe'd; existence guard is moot since hooks only exist where voice is on).
2. Port ALL FIVE reference hooks: Stop, Notification, PreToolUse(matcher=AskUserQuestion), PermissionRequest, UserPromptSubmit. All fire the same command; the binary dispatches by hook_event_name, so including all is free + faithful.
3. .claude/settings.local.json always-allow write: ACCEPT, no handling. It's Claude Code's standard per-project runtime local-override file, never written into this nix tree by the build; gitignoring is a per-project concern. (Map 'Not yet specified' item closed.)

MERGE (verified): upstream programs.claude-code.settings uses pkgs.formats.json {} (modules/programs/claude-code.nix:26-27) — freeform recursive-merge type. So voice.nix's settings.hooks MERGES by key with the existing WorktreeCreate/WorktreeRemove block in home-modules/settings/programs/claude-code.nix. No clobber.

COMPOSITION: voice.nix (darwin feature module, mirrors music/llm) — options.features.voice.enable + config = mkIf cfg.enable { homebrew.casks=["superwhisper"]; user.programs.claude-code.settings.hooks = {...}; }. Contributes hooks unconditionally w.r.t. claude-code and NEVER sets claude-code.enable -> inert where claude-code off (settings.json write is enable-gated). Wire in darwin-modules/features/default.nix: import ./voice.nix + voice.enable = machine.features.voice. Register 'voice' in nebula.features + spectre/ash host lists (per nix-4t3.2).

Statusline SuperWhisper indicator is OUT OF SCOPE here -> nix-4t3.5 (lands in voice.nix at build time).

title: Design the voice darwin module: cask install + Claude Code hook wiring
